synagogues.name: Jerusalem Great Synagogue
synagogues.anglo_programming: Services accessible to English speakers

synagogues.name: Chabad Center Jerusalem
synagogues.anglo_programming: Torah classes, Shabbat services

Jerusalem.notes: Jerusalem has numerous Orthodox synagogues and Chabad centers catering to English speakers. The city is a religious center with significant Anglo community presence.

synagogues.name: Tel Aviv International Synagogue (TAIS)
synagogues.anglo_programming: Full English services, diverse international community from 30+ countries

synagogues.name: Chabad on the Coast
synagogues.anglo_programming: Shabbat services, holiday meals, Torah classes, young professional events

synagogues.name: North Central Synagogue (NCS)
synagogues.anglo_programming: Community center with English-Hebrew programming for diverse internationals

synagogues.name: Beit Daniel
synagogues.neighborhood: Northern Tel Aviv, 62 Bnei Dan Street

synagogues.name: Kehilat Halev
synagogues.anglo_programming: Progressive community programming

synagogues.name: Kehillat Sinai
synagogues.anglo_programming: Diverse community including olim

synagogues.name: Yachad
synagogues.anglo_programming: Liberal Orthodox community

Tel Aviv.notes: Tel Aviv has the largest concentration of English-friendly synagogues in Israel, with full English services available at TAIS and Chabad on the Coast. Reform and Conservative options available for egalitarian services.

synagogues.name: Maor Yehuda Synagogue
synagogues.neighborhood: Ramat Almogi, 13A Blitental Street, Mount Carmel
synagogues.anglo_programming: Community for Technion faculty, new immigrants, diverse backgrounds

synagogues.name: Or Hadash
synagogues.anglo_programming: Educational outreach program, accessible to new immigrants

synagogues.name: Chabad Haifa
synagogues.anglo_programming: Torah classes, synagogue services

Haifa.notes: Haifa has several modern communities catering to English speakers, particularly around the Technion and Mount Carmel areas. Or Hadash is the largest Reform synagogue in northern Israel.

synagogues.name: Yavne Shul
synagogues.anglo_programming: English-speaking minyan standard

Ra'anana.notes: Ra'anana has one of Israel's largest and most established Anglo communities. English-speaking minyanim are the norm rather than exception. Multiple shuls across the religious spectrum with active English-language shiurim and learning programs.

synagogues.name: Kehillat Menorat Hamaor
synagogues.anglo_programming: Community founded by South African and Anglo families, about 18 years established

synagogues.name: Kehillat Nofei Hashemesh
synagogues.anglo_programming: 50% native English speakers, led by Rabbi Shalom Rosner

Modi'in.notes: Modi'in is considered one of the premier Anglo communities in Israel. The religious community is diverse, ranging from modern Orthodox to national-religious. Multiple neighborhoods with established Anglo populations and thriving synagogue communities.

synagogues.name: Kehillat Menorat Hamaor
synagogues.anglo_programming: Founded by South African and Anglo families

synagogues.name: Kehillat Nofei Hashemesh
synagogues.anglo_programming: Significant English-speaking membership

Beit Shemesh.notes: Beit Shemesh, particularly Ramat Beit Shemesh, is a booming city with one of Israel's largest English-speaking communities. Dozens of shuls across every hashkafa with abundant English-speaking minyanim. Home to hundreds of Anglo families in neighborhoods like Nofim and Moreshet.

Herzliya.notes: Limited specific data on Anglo-friendly synagogues in Herzliya. The city has religious infrastructure but Anglo community focus is less documented compared to other central locations.

synagogues.name: Young Israel of North Netanya
synagogues.anglo_programming: English-speaking multinational community by the sea

synagogues.name: New Synagogue of Netanya
synagogues.anglo_programming: Daily minyanim, shiurim in English, kiddushim, cultural programs, 50+ years established

synagogues.name: SNAC (South Netanya Anglo Congregation)
synagogues.anglo_programming: Anglo-focused community

synagogues.name: Tefilat Yamim
synagogues.neighborhood: Beni Berman Street, Ir Yamim area
synagogues.anglo_programming: All services and speeches in English for English-speaking communities

Netanya.notes: Netanya has the greatest concentration of English speakers among the coastal cities. Young Israel of North Netanya and SNAC are particularly well-established Anglo communities. Multiple options for English-language services.

Ashdod.notes: Ashdod has significant Jewish communities, particularly Georgian communities. Limited specific data on Anglo-friendly synagogues compared to other major cities.

synagogues.name: Kehilat Netzach Yisrael
synagogues.anglo_programming: Only non-Orthodox synagogue in Ashkelon, egalitarian and family-oriented

synagogues.name: Central Synagogue of Afridar
synagogues.anglo_programming: Mix of Hebrew and English speakers, popular with US, Canadian, South African, and UK expats

Ashkelon.notes: Ashkelon has English-speaking options including the only non-Orthodox synagogue in the city. The Central Synagogue of Afridar is popular with Anglo expats from Western countries.

synagogues.name: Chabad of English Speakers in Be'er Sheva
synagogues.anglo_programming: Torah classes, Shabbat meals, holiday celebrations

synagogues.name: Eshel Avraham
synagogues.anglo_programming: Founded by North American immigrants

Beer Sheva.notes: Beer Sheva, with ~300 synagogues, has a few Anglo-oriented congregations. The city has attracted Anglos seeking traditional but egalitarian approaches. Chabad has strong presence serving English speakers.

Petah Tikva.notes: Petah Tikva has many synagogues but limited specific data available on Anglo-friendly congregations. Further research needed.

Kfar Saba.notes: Kfar Saba has 105 synagogues but limited specific data on English-speaking communities. Further research needed.

synagogues.name: Congregation Adat Shalom – Emanuel
synagogues.anglo_programming: About 160 families, established 1970, North American immigrant foundation, diverse international membership

synagogues.name: Beit Chatam
synagogues.anglo_programming: Predominantly English-speaking membership, English shiurim and programs

synagogues.name: Chatam Shul
synagogues.anglo_programming: American yeshivish style, full-service English-speaking community

Rehovot.notes: Rehovot has notable English-speaking communities, particularly through Adat Shalom which serves diverse immigrants and native Israelis.

synagogues.name: Congregation Adat Shalom – Emanuel
synagogues.anglo_programming: Serves diverse communities including immigrants

Rishon LeZion.notes: Rishon LeZion has attracted significant English-speaking immigration and has various congregations serving English speakers in the greater region.

Efrat.notes: Efrat is a significant Anglo community location in the West Bank settlement with multiple synagogues. Further specific data needed on individual congregations and English-language services.

Maale Adumim.notes: Ma'ale Adumim, a West Bank settlement east of Jerusalem with over 40 synagogues and several yeshivas. While not as Anglo-heavy as Efrat or Ramat Beit Shemesh, it has a significant English-speaking segment, especially in national-religious areas.

Givat Shmuel.notes: Givat Shmuel, wedged between Ramat Gan and Petah Tikva, has a growing Anglo community with dedicated Facebook groups and WhatsApp chats for English speakers. While smaller than Ra'anana, it has a significant and active Anglo presence.

Ramat Gan.notes: Ramat Gan has 112 synagogues, two yeshivot, and a Kabbalah Center. Limited specific data on English-friendly congregations available.

Givatayim.notes: Givatayim is part of the Tel Aviv metropolitan region with religious infrastructure but limited specific data on English-speaking communities.

Holon.notes: Holon is part of the Tel Aviv metropolitan region. Limited specific data on Anglo-friendly synagogues available.

Bat Yam.notes: Bat Yam has multiple synagogues including ultra-Orthodox and Chabad communities. Reform option available for egalitarian services.

Bnei Brak.notes: Bnei Brak is primarily a Haredi (ultra-Orthodox) city. Limited data on English-friendly congregations. Several Hasidic synagogues and yeshivas dominate the religious landscape.

synagogues.name: Darchei Noam
synagogues.anglo_programming: Serves Ramat HaSharon, Herzliya, and northern Tel Aviv neighborhoods

Ramat HaSharon.notes: Ramat HaSharon has approximately 16 synagogues with nearly half concentrated in Neve Magen area. Established as a moshava in 1923 by Polish olim with modern religious infrastructure.

Hod HaSharon.notes: Hod HaSharon (Splendor of Sharon) is located southeast of Ra'anana. Limited specific data on English-friendly congregations, though Chabad centers are present.

Lod.notes: Lod is an ancient city with significant religious history. Limited specific data on contemporary English-friendly synagogues.

Ramla.notes: Ramla is a mixed Muslim-Jewish-Christian city with ancient religious sites. Limited data on contemporary English-friendly Jewish congregations.

Nazareth.notes: Nazareth is primarily a Christian Arab city with limited Jewish population. The Synagogue Church is built on ruins of ancient synagogue. Note: Predominantly non-Jewish community.

Acre.notes: Acre is a mixed Arab-Jewish port city in the north with historical Jewish presence. Limited data on contemporary English-friendly synagogues.

Tiberias.notes: Tiberias, on the Sea of Galilee, is a historic and religious city. Hammat Tiberias ruins include ancient synagogues. Limited data on contemporary English-friendly congregations.

Safed.notes: Safed is a spiritual center for Jewish mysticism and Kabbalah since the 16th century. Multiple historic Sephardic synagogues survive from this period. Limited contemporary English-friendly programming documented.

Karmiel.notes: Karmiel is a city in the north. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Nahariya.notes: Nahariya is a northern coastal city. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

synagogues.name: Chabad Kiryat Shmona
synagogues.anglo_programming: Torah classes, synagogue services

Kiryat Shmona.notes: Kiryat Shmona is Israel's northernmost city near the Lebanon border. Chabad center serves the community. Limited data on additional English-friendly congregations.

synagogues.name: Chabad of Kiryat Ata
synagogues.anglo_programming: Torah classes, synagogue services

Kiryat Ata.notes: Kiryat Ata is one of the Krayot (cluster of cities) in Haifa Bay area. Founded 1925. Chabad center provides religious services.

Kiryat Bialik.notes: Kiryat Bialik is one of the Krayot cluster in Haifa Bay, founded 1934. Growing Anglo community with organized networks but limited specific documented English-friendly congregations.

Kiryat Motzkin.notes: Kiryat Motzkin is one of the Krayot cluster in Haifa Bay. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Kiryat Yam.notes: Kiryat Yam (City of the Sea) is one of the Krayot cluster, founded 1946. Limited specific data on English-friendly congregations documented.

Yokneam.notes: Yokneam is a city in the north. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

synagogues.name: Chabad of Afula
synagogues.anglo_programming: Led by Rabbi Shlomo Segal

Afula.notes: Afula is in the Jezreel Valley. Has American-led Chabad center. Rabbi Menachem Gold, who has lived in Afula for 25 years, serves as Vice Mayor.

Beit She'an.notes: Beit She'an Valley location. Limited specific data on contemporary English-friendly congregations, though Amakim email network serves the broader region.

Dimona.notes: Dimona is a southern city. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Arad.notes: Arad is a southern desert city. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

synagogues.name: Chabad of Sderot
synagogues.anglo_programming: Led by Rabbi Moshe Zeev Pizam

Sderot.notes: Sderot is a southern city near Gaza. Chabad center serves the community. Limited data on other English-friendly congregations.

Ofakim.notes: Ofakim is a southern city. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available. Community receives humanitarian assistance programs.

synagogues.name: Chabad of Kiryat Gat
synagogues.anglo_programming: Led by Rabbi Moshe Havlin

Kiryat Gat.notes: Kiryat Gat is a southern city. Chabad center serves the community. Limited data on other English-friendly congregations.

Kiryat Malachi.notes: Kiryat Malachi is 17km northeast of Ashkelon. Chabad center serves the community.

Or Akiva.notes: Or Akiva is in the Haifa District. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Yavne.notes: Yavne is historically significant for Jewish tradition, center of rabbinic Judaism after Second Temple destruction. Population ~57,725 (2023). Limited contemporary English-friendly programming data.

Nes Ziona.notes: Ness Ziona is a historic city (population ~47,534 in 2023) with Great Synagogue built in 1920s. Limited contemporary English-friendly programming data.

Rosh HaAyin.notes: Rosh HaAyin is in the Central District. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Shoham.notes: Shoham is located between Rosh HaAyin and Elad. Limited specific data on English-friendly synagogues available.

Modiin Illit.notes: Modi'in Illit is a Haredi settlement in the West Bank, midway between Jerusalem and Tel Aviv. Approximately 80 synagogues serve the primarily ultra-Orthodox population. Limited English-friendly congregation data available.

Elad.notes: Elad is Israel's only officially designated religious municipality, located in the Center District between Rosh HaAyin and Shoham. Religious infrastructure dominates; limited English-friendly congregation data available.
